arts.ttl: Mudryk was backed to win Ballon d’Or by Chelsea
arts.sum: Chelsea published the statement following the shock news: “Chelsea Football Club can confirm the Football Association recently contacted our player Mykhailo Mudryk concerning an adverse finding in a routine urine test. Chelsea confirmed on Tuesday that they had been contacted by the Football Association (FA) after an "adverse finding in a routine urine test" provided by the youngster.

arts.ttl: Chelsea transfer news: Blues make SHOCK Joao Felix decision
arts.sum: Felix's future at Chelsea could be bolstered, however, as Mykhailo Mudryk reportedly tested positive for banned substance meldonium, in what was shock news yesterday. Following news of Mudryk’s provisional doping suspension, Chelsea said: “Chelsea Football Club can confirm the Football Association recently contacted our player Mykhailo Mudryk concerning an adverse finding in a routine urine test.

arts.ttl: Manchester United sent fresh Jadon Sancho verdict as Chelsea star makes feelings crystal clear
arts.sum: football.london understands Chelsea have an obligation to buy Sancho at the end of the season for around £25million and that certainly feels like good business going on the winger's performances this campaign. Former Man Utd star Gary Pallister believes the Red Devils were right to let go of Sancho in the summer, although he admitted the England international looks like a "different player" under Enzo Maresca.
